note: Numbers produced by evals/eval_extraction_synthetic.py; never hand-typed.

decision.reasoning: local_ocr has fewer false_incidents (4 vs 9) AND fewer chars_to_type (3264 vs 4902). Neither reader achieves false_incident=0 (S/A invariant). Per READER_DECISION.md tie-break rule: lower false_incident + lower chars_to_type + zero cost → local_ocr.
decision.vlm_verdict: REJECTED — false_incident=9 > local_ocr=4; VLM inserts hallucinated incidents on degraded scan sheets

frozen_thresholds_for_test.note: test split must be run ONCE; any threshold change requires a new val→freeze→test cycle

bressay_status_at_calibration.reason: VLM column unavailable (Ollama not running at BRESSAY eval time; see docs/eval_bressay_baseline.json)

test_result.protocol: test split run ONCE (DATASET_CONTRACT §10); thresholds frozen via commit

observations_not_thresholded.legacy_refusal_metric_note: Historical metric; not equivalent to safe_illegible_refusal_rate and not used as release evidence.

bressay.note: Directional only; the historical run did not authenticate a BRESSAY manifest, runtime, language pack or predeclared CER tolerance.
